meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Master SaaS Development for Students
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap in your SaaS knowledge using Git and AI. A step-by-step beginner guide to building your first portfolio project with Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the lack of SaaS knowledge requires moving beyond theory into active repository management. By combining Git version control with AI-assisted coding tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate boilerplate, debug complex logic, and deploy production-ready features, turning your first student project into a professional-grade SaaS application.
content.intro:
The blank editor stares back at you, a silent testament to the gap between knowing how to code and knowing how to build a SaaS product. You have the ambition, but the architecture of a real-world application feels like a labyrinth. You aren't just learning syntax anymore; you are learning the architecture of the internet.

Relief arrives when you stop treating code as static text and start treating it as a dynamic evolution managed by AI. By integrating Git into your workflow with the precision of an engineer, you transform from a student into a builder. This guide bridges the gap, turning your local repository into a launchpad for your career.

steps.title: Initialize Your Repository with Purpose
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, set up a professional Git structure. This ensures your project is scalable and tracks your progress as a developer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional SaaS project structure using Next.js, including folders for components, hooks, services, and utils. Initialize a .gitignore file tailored for production environments.

steps.title: Automate Feature Scaffolding
steps.description: Stop manually creating files. Use AI to scaffold core features based on your requirements, ensuring best practices in folder organization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a user authentication flow including a sign-up and login page. Use Tailwind CSS for styling and ensure the components are modular.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'scaffold auth pages with tailwind'

steps.title: Debug Logic with Contextual Awareness
steps.description: When you hit a bug, leverage AI to analyze your entire repository context rather than just a single snippet.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current authentication implementation. Identify potential security risks in the login logic and suggest a hardened implementation.

steps.title: Implement Version Control Commit Hooks
steps.description: Ensure every commit meets professional standards by automating code linting and testing before the code is saved to Git.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a husky configuration that runs ESLint and Prettier on every pre-commit hook to ensure clean code.

steps.title: Deploy to Production with Confidence
steps.description: Move your local project to a live environment, turning your Git commits into a functioning web application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file that sets up environment variables and build commands for a production SaaS app.

pro_tips: Always use atomic commits: link every Git commit to a specific feature or fix to make your portfolio look professional to hiring managers.
Use AI to write your README.md files; a well-documented repo is worth more than a complex codebase in a job interview.
Treat your Git history as a story of your learning journey; never squash commits until you are ready to push to production.
Leverage Claude Code to write unit tests for your functions; it forces you to understand the edge cases of your own code.
Automate your environment variable management using AI templates to avoid committing secrets to your public repositories.
resources: Git Best Practices for SaaS: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, focus on adding a 'Database' layer and integrating a payment provider like Stripe to complete your full-stack SaaS portfolio piece.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the roadmap; the only thing missing is your execution. Don't let your code sit on your local machine—push it, ship it, and build your reputation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
